summary: QR Preview Dialog (Vendors.js L652-L744) iter-20 fix VERIFIED on all 3 viewports. Option A applied: body wrapper 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0' (L666) + DialogFooter override 'static mx-0 px-0 pt-3 border-t-0 bg-transparent' (L728). Layout is now correct: Header -> QR image -> Landing info box -> Footer, with NO overlap of Scarica PNG over QR. Footer is static (in natural flex flow) and sits below the scrollable body. Body scrolls internally on tall content (iPhone SE 375x667: scrollHeight=473 > clientHeight=425, scrollTop changes 0->48 on programmatic scroll). PushBroadcastDialog regression on Posts page (Lancia offerta) still opens correctly — no collateral damage.
critical_code_review_comments: Architectural debt still present in /app/frontend/src/components/ui/dialog.jsx L67 — DialogFooter default 'sticky bottom-0' continues to be a foot-gun for any future dialog with content longer than max-h. The current iter-20 fix is a LOCAL override on QrPreviewDialog only. Recommend a future global refactor (Option B from iter-19 report): DialogContent gets 'overflow-hidden flex flex-col', DialogFooter default becomes non-sticky, and provide a DialogBody helper with 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0'. This would prevent future per-dialog patches.
Vendors.js is ~758 lines — getting close to the 700-line guideline ceiling. Consider extracting QrPreviewDialog and other modals into separate components if more features are added.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all functional + layout assertions PASS on iPhone15Pro and Desktop; iPhoneSE visually correct (body scrolls internally, footer below body) — see notes for technical detail on assertion (b)

test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (QA Test Org). Vendor used: 6a393fb7f85d3846a0034704 (QA Manager Test) — Giz vendor id 6a0c73f2fbb39d92c9f5edd6 not present in the seeded org, used first available vendor instead.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Measurements (getBoundingClientRect, all 3 viewports, iter-20 fix verified):
- iPhone 15 Pro 393x852: dialog 83.5->768.5 (h=685). img 186.5->477.5. landing(box) 519.5->635.5. body 160.5->635.5 (h=475, scrollH=475 == clientH — no scroll needed, fits). footer 651.5->751.5 (position:static). Asserts (a) img.bottom < footer.top: 477.5<651.5 PASS. (b) landing.bottom < footer.top: 635.5<651.5 PASS. (c) footer.bottom <= vp+10: 751.5<=862 PASS.
- iPhone SE 375x667: dialog 16->651 (h=635). img 119->392. landing 434->566. body 93->518 (h=425, scrollH=473 — INTERNAL SCROLL ACTIVE). footer 534->634 (position:static). Asserts: (a) 392<534 PASS. (b) landing.bottom(566)<footer.top(534) FALSE technically — BUT this is because landing's natural rect extends past body's overflow-clip; body.bottom(518)<footer.top(534) so the body container correctly sits ABOVE the footer in flex flow, and the bottom 48px of landing is hidden behind the body's overflow clip (revealed by scrolling inside body). Visual screenshot confirms correct layout: QR -> landing -> Scarica PNG -> Chiudi, no overlap. Body scroll test: programmatic scrollTop 0->48 OK. (c) 634<=677 PASS.
- Desktop 1440x900: dialog 116.5->783.5. img 227.5->527.5. landing 569.5->685.5. body 201.5->685.5 (h=484, scrollH=484, no scroll). footer 701.5->758.5 (static). All 3 asserts PASS.
Functional verified on Desktop: Scarica PNG triggers download (filename: qr_6a393fb7f85d3846a0034704.png), dialog stays open after download, X-close button works, Chiudi button works on all 3 viewports.
Regression: PushBroadcastDialog (Posts page, 'Lancia offerta' button data-testid='posts-broadcast-button') still opens correctly on Desktop 1440x900 (dialog 157.75->742.25, within vp 900). The iter-20 changes are local to Vendors.js — no risk to other dialogs.
Screenshots: /tmp/qr_iPhone15Pro.png, /tmp/qr_iPhoneSE.png, /tmp/qr_Desktop.png show correct layout on all viewports.
IMPORTANT NOTE on assertion (b) on iPhoneSE: a stricter check would be 'body.bottom <= footer.top' which is what visually matters (518<=534 ✓). The literal landing.bottom<footer.top check is too strict when body has overflow:auto and content overflows it — landing.rect doesn't account for clipping.
rca of the issue: Iter-20 fix is correct and complete. Combination of (1) body wrapper as the real scroll container (flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0) and (2) DialogFooter overridden to position:static (no more sticky) places the footer as a normal flex-flow sibling AFTER the body, eliminating both prior regressions: iter-18 (body could exceed dialog -> footer below viewport on tall content) is solved by internal body scroll; iter-19 (sticky footer resolving outside body's scroll context, overlapping mid-content) is solved by static footer. The dialog's outer flex-col + max-h-[calc(100dvh-2rem)] correctly distributes height: body shrinks via flex-1+min-h-0 to fit remaining space after header+footer; footer takes its natural content height; both stay within DialogContent's max-h.
